focus: Produce the implementation sequencing plan for the corrected contract: safest patch order, coupled slices, verification gates, and wrong-sequence breakage risks.

summary: Rewrite phase-013 packet docs before runtime changes so implementation and verification align to the workflow-owned synthesis contract rather than the stale reducer-local/custom-column contract.

The safest first runtime slice after the doc rewrite is a pure extractor/renderer plus unit tests, because existing workflows already pin delta paths and enforce canonical iteration artifacts before reduction.

Config/CLI plumbing and workflow invocation should ship together after the extractor exists: add top-level resourceMap.emit, --no-resource-map, and mode-parity updates across all four YAMLs before enabling step_emit_resource_map.
The final synthesis emitter must reuse the already-resolved artifact_dir; late resolveArtifactRoot calls can allocate a sibling child-phase packet and misplace resource-map.md.
Wrong-sequence breakage is concentrated in three places: stale packet acceptance criteria, mode-skew between auto and confirm, and child-phase artifact drift when paths are re-resolved late.
Verification should escalate from packet validation to unit tests to mode-parity checks to end-to-end manual loop runs instead of jumping straight to full integration.
Do not land runtime changes before the packet-doc rewrite; the current packet still encodes the wrong contract.
Do not ship only auto-mode or only confirm-mode emitter changes; both modes share the same packet contract and partial rollout would create behavioral drift.
